blocks.text: DTC P16F3: Starter cut Relay 1 voltage low

The starter relay drive circuit consists of two starter relays connected in series (starter cut relay 1 and starter cut relay 2) to prevent the starter from continuing to rotate due to a starter relay ON failure. A diagnostic line (STRLD DIAG line) is provided to monitor the voltage between the switch contacts of these two starter relays, and the AD values are captured in the PGM-FI ECU. The PGM-FI ECU is configured to detect diagnostic line disconnections and starter relay switch contact failures. In addition, a voltage monitoring circuit for the output terminals of the starter relay (ST CUT RLY1 CL- line and ST CUT RLY2 CL- line) is provided as a condition for detecting a failure. When the starter cut relay 1 drive instruction is OFF, if the ST CUT RLY1 CL- pin voltage remains Lo for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch

blocks.text: Starter cut When the drive instruction for relay 1 is OFF and the ST CUT RLY1 CL- pin voltage remains in the Lo state for 5 seconds or longer.

items.text: Ground fault between PGM-FI ECU and starter cut relay 1 code (ST CUT RLY1 CL- line)
Disconnection of code between PGM-FI ECU and starter cut relay 1 (ST CUT RLY1 CL- line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
